text:
{
  "title": "Activity-Adjusted Hydration Reserve",
  "group_name": "activity_adjusted_hydration_reserve",
  "family": "contextual_hydration_balance",
  "summary": "Estimate whether each student's fluid intake is sufficient for the water demand implied by their body context and exertion pattern, separating well-supported active lifestyles from exertion-with-deficit and low-demand excess-intake profiles.",
  "depends_on": [],
  "strategy": "Create a deterministic hydration-demand estimate in liters: baseline_need = 3.0 for gender='male', 2.2 for gender='female', and 2.6 for gender='other' or missing; activity_addon = 0.6 * max(exercise_duration,0) / 60 + 0.15 * max(step_count - 8000,0) / 5000 + 0.00015 * max(calorie_expenditure - train_median_calorie_expenditure,0); smoking_alcohol_addon = 0.25 for 'yes', 0.10 for 'occasional', and 0 for 'no' or missing; estimated_need = baseline_need + activity_addon + smoking_alcohol_addon. Derive hydration_gap = water_intake - estimated_need, hydration_ratio = water_intake / estimated_need clipped to [0,2], activity_water_pressure = activity_addon / max(water_intake,0.25), and hydration_tier using ratio bins: missing water_intake -> 'unknown', <0.55 -> 'severe_deficit', <0.80 -> 'deficit', <1.25 -> 'adequate', <1.60 -> 'high', otherwise 'excess'. Numeric missing activity drivers contribute zero addon rather than imputed exertion; negative or impossible values are clipped at zero. Guideline context: https://www.mayoclinic.org/healthy-lifestyle/nutrition-and-healthy-eating/in-depth/water/art-20044256 and https://www.nata.org/sites/default/files/2025-08/FluidReplacementsForAthletes.pdf.",
  "expected_signal": "Water intake alone may be ambiguous, while intake relative to activity and exertion can distinguish fit students who sustain high output with adequate hydration from at-risk or unhealthy students whose fluid intake is low for their activity load or unusually high despite low demand.",
  "risk": "The constants are approximate because the data lacks body weight, climate, sweat rate, and whether water_intake includes food-derived fluids, so this group may be partly redundant with existing activity and water features or unstable if the synthetic target did not encode hydration-demand relationships."
}
